plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-216 RP06.P05.M02.S15-RP06.P05.M04.S12 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-216 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP06.P05.M02 type/shape tail, the full M03 primary implementation fixture-case slice (including stable ID check and replay command rows), and the M04 secondary workflow slice head (40 rows across three sections) with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-217 handoff, without opening replay runtime, AI runtime dispatch, UI runtime execution, build runtime dispatch, DMS runtime dispatch, Hermes runtime receipts, review/approval route runtime, state writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, workflow persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-216 is a descriptor-only DMS-core fixture-case-slice pack (Risk B, 40 RP06 units, RP06.P05.M02.S15-RP06.P05.M04.S12) that follows the established CP198-CP215 accumulating pattern. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported: (1) DMS_CORE_CP216_PACK_BINDING, _REQUIREMENTS, and _NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION match the baseline exactly (risk B, 40 units, first/last unit IDs, and deliverable distribution test:6/hermes_evidence:2/implementation:13/fixture:13/claude_review:2/security_audit:4, plus micro-phase counts M02:6/M03:22/M04:12); (2) all 7 required_public_exports (createDmsCoreCp216FixtureCaseSliceCaseSet/Descriptor, validateDmsCoreCp216Coverage, validateDmsCoreCp216FixtureCaseSliceDescriptor, createDmsCoreCp216HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff) exist, are re-exported via index.js, exercised by 3 dedicated tests in packages/dms/test/model.test.js, and asserted by scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs; (3) the contract (contracts/dms-core-contract.json) projects current_pack.pack_id=CP00-216, program.current_pack_id=CP00-216, and fixture_case_slice_descriptor.descriptor=DmsCoreCp216FixtureCaseSliceDescriptor, so the validator's contract-drift checks pass; (4) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced at multiple layers (freezeCp216Result, NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, per-row descriptor_only flags) with executes_replay_runtime=false, exposes_stable_id_material=false, dispatches_ai_runtime=false, and the inherited CP215 row-extras supply every fixture-case field the validator inspects; (5) the authority boundary holds - claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, Claude packet read_only=true with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, and Hermes appears only as deterministic evidence packets/receipts (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false) not product runtime; (6) CP178+ review hardening is in place via forbidden_review_evidence (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_or_enterprise_trust_claim) and the eight-step hardened_review_sequence. Crucially the pack does NOT over-claim production readiness: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both carry production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, and all 18 recorded implementation/plan/validation commands exit 0; the descriptor-level production_ready_candidate is correctly gated on coverage.valid && slice.valid. No P0/P1 defects found. One non-blocking P3: the manifest's per-unit evidence_refs reference post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) not yet present on disk, which is the expected pending-review state explicitly documented as post-review-gated in command-evidence notes. Claude is acting as read-only independent reviewer and not final approver; final closeout still requires the post-review gated commands and human approval.

findings.title: Post-review closeout evidence artifacts referenced by manifest are not yet present (expected pending-review state)
findings.details: Each unit's evidence_refs lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-216/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with the pack's status (implemented_pending_review) and production_ready=false. command-evidence.json notes explicitly mark the normalize/receipt/adjudication/inspection steps and npm run closeout-pack:validate as post-review gated commands that run after an accepted review. No premature production_ready or enterprise-trust claim is made anywhere in the manifest, contract, or sources. This review (the C06 Claude gate) is necessary but not sufficient for closeout.
findings.recommendation: Proceed through the documented hardened_review_sequence (read_only_claude_raw_capture -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation) and obtain human final approval before any downstream pack flips production_ready or opens runtime behavior. Ensure the referenced evidence files are generated and validated at closeout so manifest evidence_refs resolve.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; expected pre-closeout state, not a defect. Does not block pack closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-216 dms core fixture case slice descriptor pack
